summary: Backend API testing for iteration 3 completed successfully. All 30 tests passed covering the new signup-first flow: student registration, student login (email OR phone), auth-required application submission, /api/applications/me endpoint, admin approve reusing existing user_id, and full E2E flow. Legacy regression tests also passed.
critical_code_review_comments: New signup-first flow correctly implemented - students must register before applying
POST /api/auth/student/register validates password length (>=6), phone format (digits only, >=7), and prevents duplicate email/phone
POST /api/auth/student/login accepts identifier (email OR phone) + password - works correctly
POST /api/applications now requires student auth (returns 401 without auth, 403 for admin)
Application payload correctly uses only academic fields (course/year/percentage/backlogs/preferred_room_type) - user data comes from session
Duplicate active application prevention works - returns 400 if user already has Pending/Approved application
GET /api/applications/me returns null for students without application, returns latest application otherwise
Admin approve correctly reuses existing user_id - no new password generated, login_credentials references 'existing signup password'
Full E2E flow works: signup → apply → admin approve → student login (with signup password) → /students/me returns room
All legacy flows still work: admin login, room CRUD, complaints, CSV exports, dashboard stats, payments config

test_credentials.student: Created via POST /api/auth/student/register with name/email/phone/password. After approval, student logs in with their ORIGINAL signup password (not a new generated one).

seed_data_creation: Tests use TEST_IT3_ prefix for all created data. 8 demo rooms seeded on startup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 3 refactored the application flow - students must now signup/login BEFORE applying. Key changes: (1) POST /api/auth/student/register creates student user, (2) POST /api/auth/student/login accepts {identifier, password} where identifier is email OR phone, (3) POST /api/applications requires student auth and only needs academic fields in body (user data from session), (4) GET /api/applications/me returns student's latest application, (5) Admin approve reuses existing user_id - no new password generated. Previous test files (test_hostel_api.py, test_iteration2_integrations.py) have tests that assume application POST is public with name/email/phone in body - those assumptions are NO LONGER VALID.

mocked_integrations_verified.razorpay: Returns enabled:false when RAZORPAY_KEY_ID not configured
mocked_integrations_verified.resend_email: Logs [EMAIL DISABLED] when RESEND_API_KEY not set - non-blocking
mocked_integrations_verified.emergent_google_oauth: Not tested in this iteration (tested in iteration 2)
